folio.label: 173v
folio.l: man to haue a good name , than for to haue grete richesses

¢ And æäfore he seitú in another place ; Do gret diligence s-
eitú Salamou~ in kepyngö of thy frend , & of thy goode name

for it shal lengere a bide w the , than any tresor , be it neuäe
so päcious ,

And certes he shulde nat be called a gentil man
æ after god & good conscience alle thynges left , ne dootú
his diligence & bisynesse , to kepen his goode name

¢ And Cassi-
dore seitú ; æ it is signe of a gentil herte , whan a man lo-
uetú & desiretú , to haue a good name

¢ And æäfore seitú Sei~t
Austyn , that æäe ben two thynges , æ arn necessarie & nedefulle
& æ is good co~science , & good loos ,

æ is to seyn ; good co~science
to thyn owne ¹sone inward , And good loos , for thy neigúbore
out ward , 675Cross in ink in the right margin.ODR
And he æ hi~ so muchel in his good co~science trostetú ,

æ he displesetú & settetú at nougút his good name , or loos ,
& rekketú nat , thougú he kepe nat his good name , nys but
a cruel cherl

¢ Sire now haue I shewed ©ow , how ©e shulden
do in getyngö richesses , & how ©e shuln vsen he~ ,

And I se wel
æ for the trust , æ ©e han in ©oure richesses , ©e wyln meeue
werre & bataille ,

I cou~seille ©ow , æ ©e bigynne no bataille ne
werre , in trust of ©oure richesses , for they ne suffisen nougút
werres to mayntene

¢ And æäfore seitú a Philosophre ; æ
man æ desiretú & wol algates han werre , shal neuäe haue
suffisaunce ,

for the richere æ he is , æe grettere despences must
he make , if he wol haue worship~ & victorie

¢ And Salamou~
seitú ; æ the gretterö richesses æ a man hatú , the moo despendours
he hatú

¢ And deere sire , al be it so , æ for ©oure richesses , ©e
moun haue muchel folk ,

©et bihouetú it nat , ne it is nat good
to bigynne werre , where as ©e moun in othere manere , haue
pees vn to ©oure ¸fit ,

for the victorie of batailles , æ ben in æés
world , litú nat in gret noumbre , or multitude of peeple , ne in
the vertu of man ,

but it litú in the wil & in the hand of ou-
re lord god almygúty

¢ And æäfore Iudas Machabeus , whicú
was goddes knygút ,

whan he shulde figúte a geyn his aduäsarie
æ hadde a gretterö noumbre , & a grettere multitude of folk & stren-
gere , than was the peeple of this Machabee ,

©et he reco~forted
his litel co~paignye , & seide rigút in this wyse

¢ As ligútly
may oure lord god almygúty , ©eue victorie to fewe folk , as
to manye folk ,

for the victorie of a bataile , cometú nat by æe
gret noumbre of peeple ,

but it cometú from oure lord god of
heuene

æäfore euäy man shulde , gretly drede
werres to bigynne ,
and by cause æ in batailles fallen manye
